overview: Iterative loop for CTI ingestion, prioritization, and operational handoff using CyberSquad personas.
goals: Convert daily OpenCTI intake into actionable decisions
Preserve structured memory across loop iterations
Support repeatable persona collaboration with auditable outputs
nonGoals: No direct destructive write operations in OpenCTI
No secret storage in repository files
successMetrics: Daily brief produced with evidence, assumptions, and confidence
Action plan includes owner and SLA for each priority item
openQuestions: Which OpenCTI entities should be auto-tagged vs manually reviewed?

stories.title: Create OpenCTI operating context for personas
stories.description: As Orion (CTI), I want a consistent operating context so future iterations can prioritize intel with less ambiguity.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `_cybersquad/_memory/opencti-context.md` with: data sources, sector focus, severity rubric, confidence rubric
Example: include one scored item showing severity + confidence
Negative case: missing confidence rubric should fail validation

stories.title: Generate daily OpenCTI intelligence brief
stories.description: As Orion (CTI) with Raven (Hunter), I want a concise daily brief so SOC and detection teams can focus on high-value threats.
acceptanceCriteria: Include at least 3 prioritized items with rationale and ATT&CK mapping where available
Example: one item maps to concrete hunt hypothesis
Negative case: item without relevance rationale should fail validation

stories.title: Create actionable handoff plan
stories.description: As Atlas (SOC) + Forge (Detection) + Patch (VulnOps), I want an action plan so operations can execute quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `outputs/opencti/action-plan.md` with sections: Now (<=24h), Next (<=72h), Later (<=30d)
Each action includes owner persona, expected outcome, and confidence score
Example: at least one detection tuning action and one vulnerability prioritization action
Negative case: action without owner or timeline should fail validation
